name: White Christmas Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces white chocolate baking bar, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
2. In a medium bowl, combine flour, brown sugar, cocoa, and salt. Using a pastry blender or fork, cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Sprinkle flour mixture with water, one tablespoon at a time, while tossing and mixing lightly with a fork. Add water gradually until the dough is just moist enough to hold together.
4. Form the dough into a ball. Flatten the ball and place it on a lightly floured surface. Roll from the center outwards into a circle about 1 1/2 inches larger than an inverted 9-inch pie pan. Fold the dough in half, then place it in the pan. Unfold; fit evenly into the pan.
5. Do not stretch the dough. Fold the edge under to form a standing rim and flute.
6. Prick the bottom and sides of the crust generously with a fork. Bake at 450 degrees for 8 to 11 minutes, or until the crust appears dry and flaky. Allow to cool completely.
7. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar and 1/4 cup cornstarch. Gradually add milk, stirring over medium heat until the mixture boils; boil for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
8. Add the white chocolate baking bar and vanilla, stirring until the bar is melted and the mixture is smooth. Pour into a large bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Allow to cool to room temperature.
9. In a small bowl, beat whipping cream with powdered sugar until soft peaks form. Beat the cooled chocolate mixture at medium speed until light and fluffy, about 1 minute. Fold in the whipped cream.
10. Spoon the filling evenly into the cooled crust. Refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight until firm.
11. Serve pie with raspberry sauce. Serves 8.
